examples: You can have it in either colour.
There is a locomotive at either end of the train, one pulling and the other pushing.
There are roses on either side of the garden.

definitions.text: One or the other of two people or things.
examples: He made me two offers, but I did not accept either.

definitions.text: Both, each of two or more.

examples: I don't like him, and I don't like her either.
I know a cheap Spanish restaurant. It's not far from here, either.

definitions.text: Introduces the first of two (or occasionally more) options or possibilities, the second (or last) of which is introduced by “or”.
examples: Either you eat your dinner or you go to your room.
You can have either potatoes or rice with that, but not both.
